description: record literal `{key: expr, ...}` inside policy expressions (Phase 4)

tests.name: single_long_field_access
tests.description: access a long-valued field from a record literal via dot

tests.name: single_string_field_access
tests.description: access a string-valued field from a record literal

tests.name: single_bool_field_as_guard
tests.description: a boolean-valued field used directly as the when guard

tests.name: multi_field_access
tests.description: access a specific field from a multi-field record literal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {a: 1, b: 2, c: 3}.b == 2 };

tests.name: nested_record_access
tests.description: access a scalar nested inside a record literal through two dots

tests.name: empty_record_has_false
tests.description: `has` on an empty record literal returns false so permit does not apply
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {} has foo };

tests.name: has_on_literal_true
tests.description: `has` on a record literal is true when the field is present
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {foo: 1} has foo };

tests.name: has_on_literal_false
tests.description: `has` on a record literal is false when the field is absent
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {foo: 1} has bar };

tests.name: order_independent_equality
tests.description: two record literals with the same fields in different orders compare equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {a: 1, b: 2} == {b: 2, a: 1} };

tests.name: equality_field_mismatch
tests.description: record literals differing in a value compare unequal

tests.name: equality_size_mismatch
tests.description: record literals of different sizes compare unequal

tests.name: string_key_bracket_access
tests.description: string-quoted key on a literal and bracket access with the same key

tests.name: literal_matches_injected_record_attr
tests.description: record literal compares equal to a record attribute injected via the eval-context API
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { principal.address == {city: "Tokyo", zip: "100"} };

tests.name: literal_mixed_types
tests.description: record literal mixing string, long and bool fields
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {s: "a", n: 1, b: true}.n + 1 == 2 };

tests.name: duplicate_key_parse_error
tests.description: repeating a key in a record literal is rejected at parse time; policy fails to load so request denies by default

tests.name: trailing_comma_accepted
tests.description: trailing comma after the last entry is accepted (matches Cedar reference)

tests.name: set_value_in_literal_accepted
tests.description: record literal with a set-valued field is evaluated and compared to a set literal

tests.name: missing_colon_parse_error
tests.description: record entry without `:` between key and value is a parse error

tests.name: missing_key_parse_error
tests.description: record entry without a key is a parse error

tests.name: like_star_escape_in_key_parse_error
tests.description: string key containing \* (only valid in like patterns) is rejected at parse time

tests.name: too_many_entries_parse_error
tests.description: exceeding NXE_CEDAR_MAX_RECORD_ENTRIES (64 unique keys) is a C-implementation-specific parse error; Cedar reference has no such limit, so FFI oracle comparison is skipped

tests.name: nesting_depth_at_limit
tests.description: record literal nested to exactly NXE_CEDAR_MAX_RECORD_DEPTH (16) is accepted by the parser
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:1}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}} has a };

tests.name: nesting_depth_exceeded_parse_error
tests.description: record literal nested deeper than NXE_CEDAR_MAX_RECORD_DEPTH (16) is a C-implementation-specific parse error; Cedar reference has no such limit, so FFI oracle comparison is skipped
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { {a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:{a:1}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}} has a };
